description: Compact human-facing status generated from paper-local status.json files. Use papers/status.json for detailed machine/audit metadata.
sort_policy: Formalized papers first, including formalized-with-caveat rows, ordered by publication year; partially formalized papers follow in publication-year order.
note_policy: main_note is intentionally sparse. Fully formalized papers have a blank note unless a source-version or proof-route note is important for a public reader.
review_count_policy: human_review counts saved human dashboard rows as reviewed/total. Agent audits are not counted as human review.

papers.title: College Admissions and the Stability of Marriage
papers.authors: D. Gale and L. S. Shapley
papers.paper_info: College Admissions and the Stability of Marriage by D. Gale and L. S. Shapley; American Mathematical Monthly, 1962.
papers.main_note: This only uses a few lines of code as its infrastructure has largely been elevated to the shared matching library.

papers.title: The Economics of Matching: Stability and Incentives
papers.paper_info: The Economics of Matching: Stability and Incentives by Alvin E. Roth; Mathematics of Operations Research, 1982.

papers.title: Competitive Auctions and Digital Goods
papers.authors: Andrew V. Goldberg, Jason D. Hartline, and Andrew Wright
papers.paper_info: Competitive Auctions and Digital Goods by Andrew V. Goldberg, Jason D. Hartline, and Andrew Wright; SODA, 2001.
papers.main_note: Theorem 8.2 follows the journal version; the broader preliminary wording is documented as refuted.

papers.title: AdWords and Generalized Online Matching
papers.authors: Aranyak Mehta, Amin Saberi, Umesh Vazirani, and Vijay V. Vazirani
papers.paper_info: AdWords and Generalized Online Matching by Aranyak Mehta, Amin Saberi, Umesh Vazirani, and Vijay V. Vazirani; Journal of the ACM, 2007.

papers.title: Driver Surge Pricing
papers.paper_info: Driver Surge Pricing by Nikhil Garg and Hamid Nazerzadeh; Management Science, 2021.

papers.title: Test-optional Policies: Overcoming Strategic Behavior and Informational Gaps
papers.paper_info: Test-optional Policies: Overcoming Strategic Behavior and Informational Gaps by Zhi Liu and Nikhil Garg; EAAMO, 2021.

papers.title: Addressing Discretization-Induced Bias in Demographic Prediction
papers.authors: Evan Dong, Aaron Schein, Yixin Wang, and Nikhil Garg
papers.paper_info: Addressing Discretization-Induced Bias in Demographic Prediction by Evan Dong, Aaron Schein, Yixin Wang, and Nikhil Garg; ACM FAccT / PNAS Nexus, 2024.

papers.title: User-item fairness tradeoffs in recommendations
papers.authors: Sophie Greenwood, Sudalakshmee Chiniah, and Nikhil Garg
papers.paper_info: User-item fairness tradeoffs in recommendations by Sophie Greenwood, Sudalakshmee Chiniah, and Nikhil Garg; NeurIPS, 2024.

papers.title: Balancing Producer Fairness and Efficiency via Bayesian Rating System Design
papers.authors: Thomas Ma, Michael S. Bernstein, Ramesh Johari, and Nikhil Garg
papers.paper_info: Balancing Producer Fairness and Efficiency via Bayesian Rating System Design by Thomas Ma, Michael S. Bernstein, Ramesh Johari, and Nikhil Garg; ICWSM, 2025.
papers.main_note: Formalization required an additional assumption that Bernoulli success probability was strictly bounded away from 0 and 1.

papers.title: Truth Revelation in Approximately Efficient Combinatorial Auctions
papers.authors: Daniel Lehmann, Liadan Ita O'Callaghan, and Yoav Shoham
papers.paper_info: Truth Revelation in Approximately Efficient Combinatorial Auctions by Daniel Lehmann, Liadan Ita O'Callaghan, and Yoav Shoham; Journal of the ACM, 2002.
papers.main_note: Greedy approximation, truthfulness, and Theorem 6.1 reductions are closed; machine-level complexity remains external.

papers.title: On Approximately Fair Allocations of Indivisible Goods
papers.authors: Richard J. Lipton, Evangelos Markakis, Elchanan Mossel, and Amin Saberi
papers.paper_info: On Approximately Fair Allocations of Indivisible Goods by Richard J. Lipton, Evangelos Markakis, Elchanan Mossel, and Amin Saberi; ACM EC, 2004.
papers.main_note: Sections 2 and 4 are closed; Section 3 has query/descent/rounded-search support. The PTAS/FPTAS runtime layer needs reusable fixed-dimension IP complexity infrastructure.

papers.title: Algorithmic Monoculture and Social Welfare
papers.paper_info: Algorithmic Monoculture and Social Welfare by Jon Kleinberg and Manish Raghavan; arXiv:2101.05853.
papers.main_note: Continuous RUM endpoints are exposed; arbitrary-size Mallows dominance and remaining paper-scope bridges remain open.

papers.title: Dropping Standardized Testing for Admissions Trades Off Information and Access
papers.authors: Nikhil Garg, Hannah Li, and Faidra Monachou
papers.paper_info: Dropping Standardized Testing for Admissions Trades Off Information and Access by Nikhil Garg, Hannah Li, and Faidra Monachou; arXiv:2010.04396 / Management Science accepted version, 2026.
papers.main_note: Main early results and selected Section 5 bridge endpoints are exposed; Bayesian-game row identifications remain open.

papers.title: Internet Advertising and the Generalized Second-Price Auction
papers.authors: Benjamin Edelman, Michael Ostrovsky, and Michael Schwarz
papers.publication: NBER Working Paper 11765 / AER 2007 publication citation
papers.paper_info: Internet Advertising and the Generalized Second-Price Auction by Benjamin Edelman, Michael Ostrovsky, and Michael Schwarz; NBER Working Paper 11765 / AER 2007 publication citation.
papers.main_note: Non-truthfulness, examples, and finite theorem endpoints are exposed with an explicit completion boundary.

papers.title: Marriage, Honesty, and Stability
papers.paper_info: Marriage, Honesty, and Stability by Nicole Immorlica and Mohammad Mahdian; MIT-LCS-TR-913 / SODA 2005.
papers.main_note: Matching preliminaries and theorem wrappers are exposed; Algorithm 4.1 and experiment constructions remain open.

papers.title: Reconciling the Accuracy-Diversity Trade-off in Recommendations
papers.authors: Kenny Peng, Manish Raghavan, Emma Pierson, Jon Kleinberg, and Nikhil Garg
papers.paper_info: Reconciling the Accuracy-Diversity Trade-off in Recommendations by Kenny Peng, Manish Raghavan, Emma Pierson, Jon Kleinberg, and Nikhil Garg; arXiv:2307.15142v1.
papers.main_note: Finite optimization and distribution checkpoints are exposed; general order-statistic certificates remain open.
